skills.description: Build Stripe commerce checkout endpoints with per-project restricted keys, Stripe App RAK setup, branded Checkout Session URLs, and Stripe CLI provisioning. Test mode is the default; live mode requires explicit user confirmation.

skills.name: subagent-driven-development
skills.description: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session

skills.name: suno-track-archiver
skills.description: Wrap a sunoapi.org music-generation call so the audio variants AND the lyrics land in `<project>/library/tracks/<yyyy-mm-dd>-<slug>/` instead of getting orphaned on suno's web UI. Use whenever you'd otherwise hit the raw Suno API by voice or chat — this skill is the only path that captures lyrics + request/response metadata next to the mp3s. Also runs in archive-only mode (taskId in, files out) to rescue runs that already happened bare-API.

skills.name: suno-track-producer
skills.description: Concept → lyrics → style string → sanitization → operator gate → Suno generation → archive → iterate, for an artist project governed by a lyric-craft bible (brand/lyric-craft.md). The writing-side complement to suno-track-archiver. Use for any "make a track / write a song / generate music on Suno / re-roll that track" request in a bible-governed repo (stacy.offline is the reference project). Refuses to run where no lyric-craft bible exists — run the craft analysis first.

skills.name: swiftui-performance-audit

skills.name: swiftui-ui-patterns

skills.name: swiftui-view-refactor
skills.description: Refactor SwiftUI view files into stable, testable structure. Use when splitting large views, tightening data flow, or cleaning Observation ownership.

skills.name: test-driven-development

skills.name: triage-autohub-runtime
skills.description: Diagnose recent AutoHub runtime incidents from logs and live health evidence, separating first causes from retry cascades, process-local database poison, credential-copy drift, platform recipient or permission failures, and intentional fallbacks. Use when AutoHub logs show repeated errors, stuck writes, webhook retry storms, MCP authentication failures, or regressions after a restart.
